reforms.description: The Strategy for Responsible Development aims to strengthen industry, innovation, and infrastructure in Poland, proposing over 700 actions to promote digitalisation and support sectors where Poland has a competitive advantage.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By enhancing innovation and infrastructure, the strategy is expected to boost productivity and economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The Strategy for Responsible Development was adopted in February 2017.
reforms.importance_rationale: This strategy represents a comprehensive approach to enhancing Poland's innovation ecosystem and economic structure, with significant implications for long-term growth.
reforms.source_quote: The Strategy for Responsible Development sets out a vision to strengthen industry, innovation and infrastructure in Poland.

reforms.description: The government plans to increase total R&D spending from 1% of GDP in 2015 to 1.7% in 2020 and 2.5% in 2030, aiming to enhance research quality and innovation capacity.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Increasing R&D funding is expected to enhance research capabilities and foster innovation, leading to higher productivity.
reforms.status_evidence: The government has set quantitative targets for R&D spending.
reforms.importance_rationale: This significant increase in R&D funding is a key element of the broader strategy to enhance Poland's innovation landscape.
reforms.source_quote: Quantitative targets include: Increase total R&D spending from 1% of GDP in 2015 to 1.7% in 2020 and 2.5% in 2030.

reforms.description: The Polish government aims to increase public spending on higher education and research by 0.5% of GDP, aligning with international standards and enhancing educational outcomes.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Investing in higher education and research is expected to improve human capital and innovation capacity, leading to higher productivity.
reforms.status_evidence: The government has indicated plans to increase funding for higher education and research.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform is a substantial investment in the education sector, which is vital for fostering innovation and economic growth.
reforms.source_quote: Additional spending on business R&D is planned to increase by 0.35% of GDP by 2020.

reforms.description: Poland is preparing a reform of procurement law and public purchasing policies to enhance competition in public procurement and increase the use of green and innovation-related criteria.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Improving public procurement processes can lead to better quality investments that support innovation and environmental efficiency.
reforms.status_evidence: A reform of procurement law and public purchasing policies is under preparation.
reforms.importance_rationale: The reform aims to significantly improve public procurement practices, which can have a broad impact on innovation and environmental outcomes.
reforms.source_quote: A reform of procurement law and public purchasing policies is under preparation. Its purpose is to enhance competition in public procurement and increase the use of green and innovation-related criteria.

reforms.description: Poland plans to develop integrated electronic procurement processes for all levels of government to improve market transparency and boost competition.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Enhancing transparency and competition in public procurement can lead to more efficient use of resources and foster innovation.
reforms.status_evidence: E-procurement will be mandatory as of 2018.
reforms.importance_rationale: The initiative represents a significant step towards modernizing public procurement, which can enhance efficiency and innovation in public spending.
reforms.source_quote: E-procurement will be mandatory as of 2018, and developing integrated electronic procurement processes for all levels of government would improve market transparency and boost competition.

reforms.description: The Polish government plans to develop more structured doctoral training with Ph.D. grant funding based on competition and stricter requirements to award a doctoral degree, aiming to improve the quality of higher education and research.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Improving the quality of doctoral training is expected to enhance the skills of researchers, thereby fostering innovation and economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The government plans to develop more structured doctoral training.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform addresses a significant gap in the quality of doctoral education, which is crucial for enhancing research capacity and innovation.
reforms.source_quote: The government plans to develop more structured doctoral training with Ph.D. grant funding based on competition and stricter requirements to award a doctoral degree.

reforms.description: The Polish government intends to enhance industry-science cooperation by giving more weight to successful commercialization in evaluations determining research funding, aiming to improve the commercialization of research results.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By incentivizing successful commercialization, the reform aims to bridge the gap between research and market application, fostering innovation and economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The government wants to create better incentives to commercialise research results.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform is significant as it aims to strengthen the link between research and industry, which is essential for enhancing innovation capacity.
reforms.source_quote: The government wants to create better incentives to commercialise research results.

reforms.description: The Polish government is considering reforms to improve the grants system for innovation, aiming to better steer funds towards innovative projects, particularly in small enterprises, and reduce bureaucratic barriers for applicants.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By streamlining the grants process and focusing on high-risk, long-term research, the reform is expected to enhance innovation and support economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The government is considering reforms to improve the grants system for innovation.
reforms.importance_rationale: The reform represents a significant change in how innovation grants are managed, aiming to enhance their effectiveness and accessibility for SMEs.
reforms.source_quote: The grants system for innovation needs to become better at steering funds towards the most innovative projects, including in small enterprises.

reforms.description: The Polish government is enhancing its loan guarantee programs for SMEs, transitioning the 'de minimis guarantee' into a permanent measure to improve access to finance for small businesses.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By providing loan guarantees, the reform aims to facilitate access to finance for SMEs, which can lead to increased investment and innovation.
reforms.status_evidence: The biggest programme, the 'de minimis guarantee', is being turned into a permanent programme.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform is significant as it aims to provide ongoing support for SMEs, enhancing their financial stability and capacity for innovation.
reforms.source_quote: The biggest programme, the 'de minimis guarantee', was introduced as a temporary measure... but it is being turned into a permanent programme.

reforms.description: The Polish Development Fund was remodelled to serve as an umbrella organization for various government agencies, streamlining innovation and investment support measures for SMEs.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The reform enhances the effectiveness of support measures for SMEs, potentially leading to increased innovation and productivity.
reforms.status_evidence: The Polish Development Fund was remodelled in 2016 to improve support for innovation and investment.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant structural change in how innovation support is organized, aiming to enhance the overall effectiveness of public support for SMEs.
reforms.source_quote: The Polish Development Fund was remodelled in 2016 to become an umbrella organisation for the development bank and a number of government agencies responsible for SMEs.

reforms.description: The Polish government plans to establish a Foundation of the Industry of the Future to facilitate digitalisation and process innovation in the industrial sector, aiming to enhance the innovation ecosystem.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By fostering digitalisation and innovation in industry, the initiative is expected to enhance productivity and competitiveness in the sector.
reforms.status_evidence: The government plans to set up a Foundation of the Industry of the Future.
reforms.importance_rationale: The establishment of the foundation represents a significant step towards enhancing the innovation ecosystem in Poland, particularly in the industrial sector.
reforms.source_quote: The government also plans to set up a Foundation of the Industry of the Future to facilitate digitalisation and process innovation in the industrial sector.

reforms.description: The Polish government is working to improve the coordination of national and regional innovation policies through the EU smart specialisation strategy, which focuses on areas with strong innovation capacity.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Better coordination of innovation policies is expected to enhance the effectiveness of innovation support and leverage regional strengths.
reforms.status_evidence: The government is working with the regions to develop quantitative indicators to evaluate smart specialisations.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform aims to enhance the strategic alignment of innovation efforts across different levels of government, which is crucial for effective innovation policy.
reforms.source_quote: Thanks to the EU smart specialisation strategy there now seems to be better coordination between the national and regional innovation policies.

reforms.description: The Polish government plans to develop venture capital financing for various stages of early firm development, providing almost PLN 3 billion of public funds to stimulate high-tech start-ups.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Increased venture capital funding is expected to enhance the growth of innovative firms, thereby boosting productivity and economic development.
reforms.status_evidence: The government plans to develop venture capital financing for various stages of early firm development.
reforms.importance_rationale: This initiative represents a significant investment in the startup ecosystem, aiming to catalyze innovation and entrepreneurship across the country.
reforms.source_quote: The government plans to develop venture capital financing for various stages of early firm development by providing almost PLN 3 billion of public funds.

reforms.description: The Ministry of Science is set to build a network for 35 applied research centres, centralising investments in research infrastructure, human resources, and intellectual property management to improve efficiency.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Centralising investments in research infrastructure is expected to enhance research quality and efficiency, leading to better outcomes in applied research.
reforms.status_evidence: The Ministry of Science is set to build a network for 35 applied research centres.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a structural change in the management of public research institutes, aiming to enhance the overall research landscape in Poland.
reforms.source_quote: The Ministry of Science is set to build a network for 35 applied research centres, by centralising their investments in research infrastructure.

reforms.description: The Polish government plans to simplify procedures for applying for national and EU funds for innovation support, particularly benefiting SMEs by reducing complexity and enhancing accessibility.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Simplifying access to funding will likely increase the number of innovative projects, particularly among SMEs, thereby enhancing overall productivity and economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The government is considering abandoning formal requirements as an initial filter for applicants.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant overhaul of the funding application process, aiming to enhance innovation uptake among SMEs, which is crucial for economic development.
reforms.source_quote: Simplifying procedures to apply for national and EU funds for innovation support would enhance take-up, in particular among SMEs.

reforms.description: The government plans to simplify access to innovation vouchers, which allow smaller companies to purchase R&D services from universities, thereby enhancing technology transfer.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By making it easier for SMEs to access R&D services, this reform is expected to foster innovation and improve the competitiveness of smaller firms.
reforms.status_evidence: The government plans to simplify procedures to access the innovation voucher.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform is significant as it directly impacts the ability of SMEs to engage with research institutions, enhancing their innovation capabilities.
reforms.source_quote: The government plans to simplify procedures to access the innovation voucher, which allows smaller companies to buy R&D from universities.

reforms.description: The Polish government plans to strengthen demand for innovation through public procurement by creating sufficient demand for innovative products and services, moving away from a focus solely on low prices.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By promoting innovative solutions through public procurement, the reform aims to stimulate market demand for advanced technologies, which can enhance productivity and innovation.
reforms.status_evidence: The government wants to use public procurement as an instrument to promote innovation.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant shift in procurement practices aimed at fostering innovation, which is crucial for enhancing the overall innovation ecosystem in Poland.
reforms.source_quote: The government wants to use public procurement as an instrument to promote innovation by creating sufficient demand.

reforms.description: The SOLARIS synchrotron radiation facility was inaugurated to support cutting-edge research across various scientific fields, enhancing Poland's research infrastructure.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The establishment of advanced research facilities like SOLARIS is expected to enhance research capabilities, attract talent, and foster innovation in various sectors.
reforms.status_evidence: The first Polish synchrotron radiation facility SOLARIS was inaugurated at the Jagiellonian University in Kraków in 2015.
reforms.importance_rationale: The establishment of SOLARIS represents a major investment in research infrastructure that can significantly enhance Poland's research capabilities and innovation potential.
reforms.source_quote: The first Polish synchrotron radiation facility SOLARIS was inaugurated at the Jagiellonian University in Kraków in 2015.

reforms.description: The Polish authorities have reformed the organisation of support to innovation and business RDI by introducing the Innovation Council, which engages five ministries and aims to improve coordination among various agencies and funds under a single Polish Development Fund.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Improved coordination among innovation support agencies can enhance the effectiveness of public spending and foster collaboration between firms and research institutions, leading to increased innovation.
reforms.status_evidence: The government attempted to improve coordination with the introduction of the Innovation Council.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a structural change in the organization of innovation support, aiming to streamline processes and enhance collaboration, which is crucial for fostering a more innovative economy.
reforms.source_quote: The Polish authorities have reformed the organisation of support to innovation and business RDI.

reforms.description: R&D tax incentives have been scaled up and restructured to support internal R&D investments, replacing the previous system that favored large firms and focused on acquiring existing technologies.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By broadening the scope of tax-deductible R&D spending, the reform encourages more firms to invest in their own R&D activities, which can lead to increased innovation and productivity.
reforms.status_evidence: A new R&D tax allowance that also supports internal R&D investments replaced the tax relief for acquiring new technology in 2016.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform significantly alters the landscape of R&D tax incentives, making them more accessible and beneficial for a wider range of firms, which is essential for enhancing innovation capacity.
reforms.source_quote: A new R&D tax allowance that also supports internal R&D investments replaced the tax relief for acquiring new technology in 2016.

reforms.description: The Polish government plans to increase R&D tax incentives and public support for innovation in SMEs, alongside developing the venture capital market and improving infrastructure, primarily funded by EU structural funds.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Enhancing R&D tax incentives and public support is expected to stimulate innovation and investment in SMEs, leading to productivity improvements.
reforms.status_evidence: The government plans higher R&D tax incentives along with increased public support for innovation.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant shift in Poland's approach to fostering innovation, aiming to enhance the overall capacity for R&D and technology adoption.
reforms.source_quote: In its Strategy for Responsible Development the government plans higher R&D tax incentives along with increased public support for innovation in SMEs.

reforms.description: The government plans to increase R&D spending to 1.7% of GDP by 2020, notably through more generous R&D tax incentives, to strengthen Poland's capacity to innovate.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Increasing R&D spending is expected to enhance innovation capacity, leading to higher productivity and economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The government plans to increase R&D spending to 1.7% of GDP by 2020.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant commitment to enhancing R&D funding, which is crucial for fostering innovation and improving the overall economic landscape.
reforms.source_quote: To strengthen Poland’s capacity to innovate the government also intends to increase R&D spending to 1.7% of GDP by 2020, notably through more generous R&D tax incentives.

reforms.description: The Polish government plans to develop a skills strategy involving the whole government and stakeholders to enhance education and training, particularly focusing on basic skills to improve economic opportunities and well-being.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Improving basic skills will enhance the productivity of the workforce, particularly in small and relatively unproductive firms, thereby supporting economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The government plans to develop a skills strategy with the OECD.
reforms.importance_rationale: The strategy aims to address significant skills deficiencies in the workforce, which is crucial for improving overall productivity and economic performance.
reforms.source_quote: The Polish government plans with the OECD to develop a skills strategy involving the whole government and stakeholders, including early-childhood and compulsory education.

reforms.description: The government has increased R&D tax allowances to 100% for all firms from 2018, significantly up from 30-50%. This reform aims to boost private R&D spending, particularly for small and young innovative firms that typically face liquidity constraints.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By increasing tax allowances, the reform incentivizes firms to invest more in R&D, which can lead to higher innovation and productivity.
reforms.status_evidence: The government has increased R&D tax allowances to 100% for all firms from 2018.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant structural change in the support for business R&D, aiming to enhance the overall innovation landscape in Poland.
reforms.source_quote: The government has increased R&D tax allowances to 100% for all firms from 2018 (and up to 150% for research centres), up from 30-50%.

reforms.description: The authorities are developing venture capital funding for young, innovative firms, primarily through EU funds, to support entrepreneurs at all stages of development.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By providing venture capital, the initiative aims to stimulate the growth of innovative startups, which can enhance overall economic dynamism.
reforms.status_evidence: The authorities are developing venture capital funding for young, innovative firms.
reforms.importance_rationale: This initiative is significant as it aims to fill a gap in the funding landscape for startups, which is crucial for fostering innovation.
reforms.source_quote: The authorities are developing venture capital (VC) funding for young, innovative and risky firms, mainly through EU funds.

reforms.description: The government plans to enhance industry-science collaboration by increasing funding for higher education and research, merging small universities and independent research institutes to build stronger research universities, and allowing underperforming institutions to shut down if they do not improve over time.
reforms.package_name: Higher Education and Research Funding Initiative
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Increased funding and improved collaboration between industry and academia are expected to enhance innovation capacity and productivity.
reforms.status_evidence: The government has announced plans to enhance industry-science collaboration.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant structural change in the funding and organization of higher education and research, aiming to improve the overall innovation ecosystem in Poland.
reforms.source_quote: Continue to increase funding for higher education and research over time, to merge small universities and independent research institutes to build strong research universities.

reforms.description: The government intends to introduce a simplified joint stock company to facilitate starting a business, with very low capital requirements and simplified online registration.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By lowering barriers to entry for new businesses, this reform is expected to stimulate entrepreneurship and innovation in the economy.
reforms.status_evidence: The government foresees the introduction of a simplified joint stock company.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform is a major overhaul of the business registration process, aimed at significantly enhancing the startup ecosystem in Poland.
reforms.source_quote: The idea would be to have very low capital requirements, possibly only 1 PLN, simplified registration online within 24 hours.

reforms.description: The Polish government has reformed the innovation framework based on the 2016 and 2017 innovation laws, aiming to increase public R&D spending from 1% of GDP in 2015 to 1.7% by 2020.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Increased public R&D funding is expected to enhance innovation capacity and productivity growth in the economy.
reforms.status_evidence: The government has taken many steps to strengthen the financing of business RDI.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant structural change in public funding for R&D, which is crucial for enhancing Poland's innovation capacity and economic growth.
reforms.source_quote: The Strategy for Responsible Development foresees an increase in R&D spending from 1% of GDP in 2015 to 1.7% in 2020.

reforms.description: The Polish government has increased public support for business innovation, with a focus on enhancing financing for R&D and innovation activities.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Enhanced public support for business innovation is expected to stimulate private sector R&D investments and improve overall productivity.
reforms.status_evidence: Public support for business innovation has increased significantly.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform is significant as it enhances the existing framework for business innovation, addressing key barriers to R&D investment.
reforms.source_quote: Public support for business innovation has increased, and the government has taken many steps to strengthen the financing of business RDI.

reforms.description: The 2017 innovation law further enhanced R&D tax incentives, proposing to increase tax relief for enterprises investing in R&D to 100% of incurred costs, which is expected to significantly boost private sector R&D investment.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The proposed increase in tax relief is likely to incentivize more firms to invest in R&D, fostering innovation and economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The authorities foresee a further reform of the R&D tax incentive to increase tax breaks for enterprises investing in R&D.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform is a major enhancement of the existing R&D tax incentive framework, aiming to significantly increase private sector investment in R&D.
reforms.source_quote: The 2017 innovation law entered into force in January 2018. The authorities foresee a further reform of the R&D tax incentive.

reforms.description: The Polish Development Fund (PFR) was created to support innovation and business at various stages of development, integrating multiple public institutions responsible for entrepreneurship support.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By consolidating support for innovation and entrepreneurship, the PFR aims to enhance the effectiveness of public funding and stimulate private investment.
reforms.status_evidence: The Polish Development Fund was created in April 2016.
reforms.importance_rationale: The establishment of the PFR represents a significant structural change in the way public support for innovation and entrepreneurship is organized in Poland.
reforms.source_quote: The Polish Development Fund (PFR) was created in April 2016.

reforms.description: Poland has introduced a new R&D tax relief scheme that allows for accelerated depreciation of R&D capital and extends the carry-forward option for tax allowances, aiming to boost R&D spending among SMEs and startups.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The tax relief is expected to incentivize R&D investments, thereby enhancing innovation and productivity in the economy.
reforms.status_evidence: The new R&D tax relief scheme has been put into effect to support innovative business investment.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant shift in the fiscal approach to supporting R&D, aiming to enhance the overall innovation landscape in Poland.
reforms.source_quote: The authorities further scaled up deductions in 2018, which are welcome steps, as the tax subsidies remained limited until 2016.

reforms.description: The 2016 innovation law permanently removed the tax on intellectual property income and created a temporary tax exemption for the sale of stocks of R&D-performing companies held by venture capitalists, which was later extended to 2023.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By stabilizing the tax environment for venture capital, the reform encourages investment in innovative firms, which can enhance productivity and economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The tax exemptions have been enacted and are currently in effect.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant structural change in the tax treatment of venture capital, which is crucial for fostering innovation and investment in R&D.
reforms.source_quote: The 2016 innovation law permanently removed the tax on intellectual property income and created a temporary tax exemption for the sale of stocks of R&D-performing companies.

reforms.description: The Polish Development Fund (PFR) was allocated a total budget of PLN 2.8 billion to support early-stage investments in start-ups and innovative companies through direct and indirect equity investments.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By providing substantial funding for early-stage investments, the reform aims to stimulate the growth of innovative firms, thereby enhancing overall economic productivity.
reforms.status_evidence: The PFR has begun its operations and the first call for proposals took place in January 2017.
reforms.importance_rationale: This initiative represents a major investment in the startup ecosystem, aiming to significantly enhance the capacity for innovation and entrepreneurship in Poland.
reforms.source_quote: The state-owned Polish Development Fund (PFR) also has a 2017-23 total budget of PLN 2.8 billion to make early-stage direct and indirect equity investments in start-ups and innovative companies.

reforms.description: The authorities plan to develop the 2014 'new chance policy' to prevent bankruptcies and support entrepreneurs through additional training and mentoring, which could encourage experimentation and innovation.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Enhancing training and mentoring for entrepreneurs can lead to increased innovation and business creation.
reforms.status_evidence: The authorities plan to develop the 2014 'new chance policy'.
reforms.importance_rationale: The development of this policy represents a significant effort to enhance support for entrepreneurs, which is crucial for fostering innovation.
reforms.source_quote: The authorities also plan to develop the 2014 'new chance policy' to prevent bankruptcies and support entrepreneurs through additional training and mentoring.

reforms.description: Recommendations include adjusting the provisions of the new R&D tax allowance if its take-up is low among small innovative firms, which aims to enhance the financing of innovative activities.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Adjusting tax incentives can stimulate R&D investment among small firms, leading to increased innovation.
reforms.status_evidence: If the take-up of the new R&D tax allowance is low among small innovative firms, adjust its provisions.
reforms.importance_rationale: This recommendation addresses a critical barrier to innovation financing for small firms, indicating a significant policy focus.
reforms.source_quote: If the take-up of the new R&D tax allowance is low among small innovative firms, adjust its provisions.
